question_text: Explain why the Southern Hemisphere experiences winter when the Northern Hemisphere experiences summer.
correct_answer: When the Northern Hemisphere is tilted towards the Sun (summer), the Southern Hemisphere is tilted away from the Sun (winter). The Sun's rays hit the Southern Hemisphere at a shallower angle, so less heat energy is received.
explanation: The 23.5° tilt of Earth's axis means that only one hemisphere can be tilted towards the Sun at any time. This opposite tilt causes opposite seasons in the two hemispheres.
